none: California aqueduct project : environmental impact statement.

The California Aqueduct Project aims to construct and maintain a series of power plants and associated facilities along the California Aqueduct, which stretches from northeast of San Francisco to Los Angeles. The project is led by the California Department of Water Resources and the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power. It seeks to improve water conveyance and generate hydroelectric power, thereby reducing operational costs and providing clean energy. The project will impact several counties, including Los Angeles, Kern, San Bernardino, and San Luis Obispo, and will involve the construction of recreational facilities around Pyramid and Silverwood Lakes. However, it will also lead to environmental changes, such as land inundation and potential wildlife habitat loss.

Los Angeles, Kern, San Bernardino, and San Luis Obispo counties, California
Los Angeles Department of Water and Power
Metropolitan Water District of Southern California

The California Aqueduct Project was proposed to enhance water delivery and hydroelectric power generation in a state that has long struggled with water scarcity and energy needs. While the project aimed to provide significant benefits, it faced public pushback due to concerns about environmental impacts, particularly regarding wildlife habitats and land use changes. Ultimately, the project moved forward with modifications to address some of these concerns.
